sections.paragraph:
(“booths”), [Succoth], pr.n.-

(1) of a town in the tribe of Gad, Jos 13:27 Jud. 8:5 1Ki 7:46 as to its origin, see Gen 33:17. עֵמֶק סֻכֹּות Psa 60:8 108:8 , the territory of (the city of) Succoth.

(2) a station of the Israelites in the desert, in the neighbourhood of Egypt, Exo 12:37 13:20 Num 33:5. It is hard to say what are-

(3) סֻכֹּות בְּנֹות 2Ki 17:30, [Succoth-benoth], booths of daughters, which the Babylonian colonists, who were brought to Samaria, are stated to have made for their idols. It is generally understood to be tents in which women prostituted themselves (compare קְדֵשָׂה); however, I expect that we ought to read סֻכֹּות בָּמֹות tabernacles (consecrated to idols) in high places. [This is, however, but a conjecture.] Compare בָּמָה.
